lines.en_lit: And afterward, the goat whose are performed outside as it is stated: “One goat for a sin-offering besides the sin-offering of atonement And afterward his ram and the ram of the people, and afterward portions of the sin-offering and afterward he sacrifices the daily afternoon offering.
What is the reason of Rabbi Eliezer? acts in accordance with which it is written First he performs Leviticus, and afterward he performs the book of Numbers.
And Rabbi Akiva’s reasoning that as teaches “Besides the morning burnt-offering which is the daily burnt-offering it is apparent additional offerings performed with the daily morning offering.
And Rabbi Eliezer, what does he do with this “Besides the sin-offering of atonement,” That is necessary for him for what this one, atones, that one, atones.

Rabbi Yehuda says in name of One is sacrificed with the daily morning offering, and six with the daily afternoon offering. Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says in name of Six are sacrificed with the daily morning offering and one with the daily afternoon offering.

What is the rationale of the Rabbis, Two verses are written It is written “Besides the morning burnt-offering But it is written “And he shall exit and make his burnt-offering Therefore, he performs some of them here, and some of them there,
With regard to what do disagree? Rabbi Yehuda holds performs one as it is written: “Besides the morning burnt-offering,” and then he performs the service of the day lest the High Priest become weak
And Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, holds: Since he has begun he performs six lest he be negligent with regard to the service of the day, diligent

everyone however, there is one ram In accordance with whose in accordance with Rabbi As it was taught Rabbi says: the one stated here is the same one stated in the Book of Numbers: Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says: There are two rams One that is stated here and one stated in the Book of Numbers,
What is the rationale of Rabbi As it is written: “One,” And Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, what “one”? unique one, of its flock.
And Rabbi He derives it from “Your choice vows” And Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, One obligatory and one free-will And are necessary
sanctified his hands and feet The Sages taught: “And Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting” Why does he come? to take out the spoon and the coal pan
